lines: Patient is a 65 year old male with a history of hypertension and diabetes.
He presents with a 2 day history of fever and cough.
The patient reports a temperature of 38.5 degrees Celsius at home.
He has been experiencing shortness of breath and chest pain.
The patient's vital signs are: temperature 38.2 degrees Celsius, blood pressure 90/60 mmHg, heart rate 110 beats per minute, respiratory rate 24 breaths per minute.
The physical exam reveals crackles in the lungs bilaterally.
The patient's labs are significant for a lactate level of 3.5 mmol/L.
A chest X-ray shows bilateral infiltrates.
The patient is being treated with oxygen and broad spectrum antibiotics.
The patient's creatinine level is 1.2 mg/dL.
The patient's platelet count is 80,000 cells per microliter.
The patient is being monitored closely for signs of sepsis.
The patient's MAP is 60 mmHg.
The patient is being considered for vasopressor support.
The patient's condition is being closely monitored.
The patient's family is being updated on his condition.
The patient's code status is being discussed with the family.
The patient's condition has not improved overnight.
The patient's temperature is still elevated at 38.5 degrees Celsius.
The patient's blood pressure is 80/50 mmHg.
The patient's heart rate is 120 beats per minute.
The patient's respiratory rate is 28 breaths per minute.
The patient's oxygen saturation is 88% on 4 liters of oxygen.
The patient's lactate level is still elevated at 3.2 mmol/L.
The patient's creatinine level has risen to 1.5 mg/dL.
The patient's platelet count is 70,000 cells per microliter.
The patient is being treated with vasopressors to support his blood pressure.
The patient's MAP is 55 mmHg.
The patient's condition is being closely monitored.
The patient's family is being updated on his condition.
The patient's code status is being discussed with the family.
The patient's prognosis is guarded.
The patient's treatment plan is being adjusted to include more aggressive antibiotic therapy.
The patient was admitted to the hospital on 01/01/2022 with a diagnosis of sepsis.
The patient was treated with broad spectrum antibiotics and vasopressor support.
The patient's condition improved slowly over the course of his hospital stay.
The patient's lactate level decreased to 1.5 mmol/L.
The patient's creatinine level decreased to 1.0 mg/dL.
The patient's platelet count increased to 150,000 cells per microliter.
The patient's blood pressure improved to 100/60 mmHg.
The patient's heart rate decreased to 80 beats per minute.
The patient's respiratory rate decreased to 18 breaths per minute.
The patient's oxygen saturation improved to 95% on room air.
The patient was discharged home on 01/10/2022.
The patient was instructed to follow up with his primary care physician in 1 week.
The patient was given a prescription for antibiotics to complete at home.
The patient's condition at discharge was stable.
The patient's prognosis is good.
